# SRC-101

> A Bitcoin Stamps naming and record protocol with lifecycle operations.

## Documentation location

This repository is kept as a nested local checkout at <code>src-101-docs</code> inside the Stamps, SRC-20 and SRC-101 documentation set. Its live site is https://bitcoinuniverse.github.io/src-101/ and its combined documentation home is https://bitcoinuniverse.github.io/src-20/. Keep SRC-101 parser rules separate from SRC-20 protocol and transaction-profile guidance.

## Chain and carrier

- Chain: Bitcoin
- Carrier: Bitcoin Stamps name records

## Overview

SRC-101 is a name and record system in the Bitcoin Stamps ecosystem. Its active reference indexer validates a closed set of JSON fields for deploy, mint, transfer, renew, and setrecord operations.

## State model

A deployed namespace is referenced by its deploy transaction hash. Token IDs are base64-encoded labels, and owner state has a renewal and record lifecycle. Exact field sets are operation-specific.

## Lifecycle

1. Deploy namespace settings and recipient rules.
2. Mint a base64-encoded token ID to an allowed recipient.
3. Transfer the name record by deploy hash and token ID.
4. Renew expiry or set signed BTC and ETH record data.

## Rules

- The reference parser requires p = src-101 and exact operation-specific field sets.
- p and op are normalized to uppercase by the active processor after parsing.
- tick is normalized to lowercase and a SHA3-256 ticker hash is calculated.
- tokenid is base64-encoded, normalized, and rejected when it contains forbidden special characters.
- deploy hash is required for mint, transfer, renew, and setrecord.
- Mint requires a duration in years, called dua, and has recipient, coefficient, and signature constraints.

## Field reference

- p (string, required: yes): src-101, then normalized by the processor.
- op (string, required: yes): deploy, mint, transfer, renew, or setrecord.
- hash (transaction hash, required: non-deploy): Deploy transaction reference.
- tokenid (base64 string or list, required: non-deploy): Normalized base64 name label, maximum 128 characters per value.
- toaddress (Bitcoin address, required: mint / transfer): Destination must pass Bitcoin address validation.
- dua (non-negative integer, required: mint / renew): Duration in years.
- prim (string boolean, required: mint / setrecord): Literal true or false value interpreted by the processor.
- type / data (record payload, required: setrecord): Signed record update fields.

## Primary sources

- Stampchain BTC Stamps reference indexer: https://github.com/stampchain-io/btc_stamps/blob/main/indexer/src/index_core/src101.py
- Stampchain FAQ: https://stampchain.io/faq
- Stamps official press-kit assets: https://github.com/stampchain-io/stampchain.io/tree/dev/static/img/presskit

## Operational caution

SRC-101 has strict, revision-sensitive field validation. Generate payloads from the active parser contract and regression-test them before using real names.
